verbatim: Thanks for taking the time. Quick framing before we start: I want to map how you actually do <process> today — not how it's documented, not how it should be. The goal is for us to find together where the time and frustration go, then we'll talk about what's worth changing.

Three ground rules: I'll talk less than 40% of the time; we'll go step-by-step, no skipping ahead to solutions; and if I ask something that sounds basic, it's because the obvious stuff is where most of the waste hides.

questions: Which process are we mapping today? One-line name.
Who are the people involved? (Roles only — no names.)
How often does this process run? (Per cycle / weekly / ad-hoc / on-trigger)
What triggers it to start?
What signals it's complete?
Roughly how long does one full run take, end to end?
What's the anchor event the cycle hangs on? (e.g., effective date YYYY-MM-DD)
Walk me through the workback stages by name — from anchor backwards.
For each stage, what's the approximate week offset from anchor? (Negative = before anchor.)
Tag each stage: live (active execution, no transformations may ship), prep (preparation, no disruptive change), or slack (post-cycle, transformations may ship).
Today, what stage are we in? What's the current week offset?
Confirm: anchor date is _____.
Walk me through the process step by step. For each step, tell me: what goes in, what comes out, who does it, what tools they use, roughly how long it takes.
Any step where you regularly hit a wait or rework loop?
Which step do you most wish you didn't have to do manually?
pacing_prompts: Want me to summarize what we have so far?
Anything we should slow down on?
Are we still on the part you wanted to focus on?

questions: Where does work cross between people or teams? List every handoff.
For each handoff: what format does the work cross in (file, email, ticket, conversation)?
Where do handoffs break — wrong format, missing data, late?
List every tool used in this process. (HRIS, Excel templates, Mercer/WTW survey, internal scripts, email threads — all of it.)
Which tools talk to each other automatically? Which require manual re-entry?
Which tool is the bottleneck or single-point-of-failure?
What's the most frustrating part of this process?
What's the part that quietly absorbs the most hours?
What's the part where you most often catch a mistake post-hoc?
If you could redesign one thing, what would it be?
What would happen if you didn't do this process for one cycle? (SPIN cost-of-inaction)

verbatim: OK — let me reflect back what I heard, and you tell me what I got wrong.
(Skill summarizes structured notes — Process Steps, Pain Points, Tools, Handoffs.)
Anything I missed or got wrong?
(Gap check — surface any sub-phase that wasn't covered: 'We didn't get into <X> — is that intentional or worth a follow-up?')
Next step on my side: I'll synthesize this into a current-state map and run a /diagnose pass. You'll see the diagnosis with 2-3 Quick Wins by <date>. Anything else before we close?
